commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: And the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleep, and seeing the prison doors open, he drew out his sword, and would have killed himself, supposing that the prisoners had fled. There was an earthquake, that the keeper should be roused from sleep, and the doors flew open, that he should wonder at what had happened: but these things the prisoners saw not: otherwise they would all have fled: but the keeper of the prison was about to slay himself, thinking the prisoners were escaped. But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, Do yourself no harm: for we are all here.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: The keeper was not an evil-disposed man that he thrust them into the inner prison,

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
